items.title: Arrays & Lists
items.description: List operations are the canonical introduction to O(1) vs O(n) and amortized analysis.

items.title: Hash Tables
items.description: Average O(1) vs worst-case O(n) is a textbook case study in amortized analysis.

items.title: Searching & Binary Search
items.description: Binary search is the prototypical O(log n) algorithm — concrete proof that halving the search space changes the growth class.

items.title: Sorting Algorithms
items.description: Comparison sorts have an Ω(n log n) lower bound — Big-O is also the language for proving such bounds.

items.title: Time-Space Tradeoffs
items.description: Big-O distinguishes time from space; trading one for the other is a common pattern (caches, precomputed tables).
